title: Western blotting in Chlamydomonas reinhardtii
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ddr39","text":"This protocols describe the steps to perform a western blot in Chlamydomonas reinhardtii cell lysate and supernatant samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"f0fg2","text":"Prepare cell material by cultivating in liquid media. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Centrifuge algae culture at 2000xg for 10 min Recover supernatant 00:10:00, Centrifugation time

Centrifuge algae culture at 2000xg for 10 min Remove supernatant, and re-suspend cells in lysis buffer (50 mM Tris·HCL (pH 8.0), 0.1% Triton X-100), concentrating cells 100-fold. 00:10:00, Centrifugation time

Sonicate using appropriate sonication tip. Duty cycle: 0.5 | Cycle: 1.0 s | Amplitude: 20% | Duration: 30 s Centrifuge for 15 min at 20000xg to remove cells debries and recover soluble proteins Quantificate soluble protein 00:15:00, Centrifugation time

Load 30 µg of total soluble protein (TSP) per lane in a 12% SDS-PAGE Transfer proteins to a nitrocelulose membrane Block the membrane with 5% milk solution Probe the desired protein with the specific antibodie, diluted in 5% milk solution Wash 2 times with TBST (0.2 M Tris, 1.37 M NaCl, 0.1% Tween-20, pH 7.6) Add secondary antibody if required undetermined, total solube protein per lane
creator: Joao Vitor Molino
